justification: Official PMLR proceedings page for the Gender Shades paper co-authored by Joy Buolamwini and Timnit Gebru, directly evidencing their research collaboration.
ACM Digital Library page for 'On the Dangers of Stochastic Parrots' lists Timnit Gebru and Emily Bender as co-authors of the 2021 FAccT paper.
Deadline article covering Fran Drescher joining the WGA picket line alongside WGA West President Meredith Stiehm, documenting both as prominent faces of the 2023 strikes.
Article explicitly names Fran Drescher, Meredith Stiehm, and Lisa Takeuchi Cullen as the key strike leaders and prominent faces of the 2023 Hollywood strikes.
Wikipedia article on the Bernie Sanders 2020 campaign documents Ro Khanna as a national campaign co-chair alongside Ben Cohen, Nina Turner, and Carmen Yulín Cruz.
Wikipedia's PayPal article documents the merger of Confinity (Thiel) and X.com (Musk), with Thiel becoming CEO after Musk's departure, evidencing their co-creation of PayPal.

Brookings event page for 'Beyond Productivity: The Pursuit of Purpose in the Age of AI' documents Sen. Chris Murphy and Brookings Senior Fellow Molly Kinder appearing together to discuss AI's impact on work and dignity.
Official White House press release announcing David Sacks and Michael Kratsios as co-chairs of PCAST.
Wikipedia article on David Sacks documents that he left McKinsey to join Confinity, the startup co-founded by Peter Thiel, Max Levchin, and Luke Nosek.
Wikipedia's PayPal Mafia article lists both David Sacks and Elon Musk as members of the elite group of former PayPal founders and early employees.
Schmidt Futures profile page for Tom Kalil documents his role as Chief Innovation Officer at Eric Schmidt's philanthropic organization.
Publisher page for 'Power and Progress' explicitly lists Daron Acemoglu and Simon Johnson as co-authors.
Yale Law & Policy Review page for the article explicitly lists Ganesh Sitaraman and Tejas N. Narechania as co-authors.
NPR article documents that Ganesh Sitaraman and Pete Buttigieg were Harvard classmates who co-founded the Democratic Renaissance Project progressive reading group.
Princeton University Press page for 'AI Snake Oil' explicitly lists Arvind Narayanan and Sayash Kapoor as co-authors.
